title: Deep Sea Lantern Buoyancy Lab
description: An underwater physics laboratory where the user controls the buoyancy of a submerged lantern by adjusting its internal gas pressure and temperature. A Barramundi fish swims nearby, reacting to the lantern's depth changes via fluid displacement forces. The goal is to achieve neutral buoyancy at three target depths without disturbing the fish beyond a turbulence threshold.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 that implements an underwater buoyancy physics simulation. Load BarramundiFish.glb and Lantern.glb using GLTFLoader from a CDN. Scene setup: dark blue-green underwater environment with volumetric fog (near=1, far=80). Place a subtle caustic light pattern on a sandy floor plane at y=0. Use ambient light (0x1a3a4a, intensity 0.4) and a point light inside the lantern (warm orange, intensity 2) that glows based on temperature. Camera starts at position (0, 10, 25) looking at origin, with OrbitControls limited to stay underwater. The lantern starts at y=20 (surface). The fish patrols at y=8 along a sinusoidal path. Physics: implement Archimedes' buoyancy principle. The lantern has mass=5kg and adjustable volume via gas pressure (slider 0.5-3.0 atm) and temperature (slider 200-800K). Compute buoyant volume using ideal gas law PV=nRT, then buoyancy force = fluid_density * g * volume. Apply gravity and drag force (proportional to velocity squared). Update lantern position each frame using semi-implicit Euler integration. The fish reacts to the lantern: when the lantern is within 8 units, compute displacement turbulence as a function of lantern velocity. If turbulence exceeds 0.7, the fish flees (doubles swim speed away from lantern). Display a turbulence meter bar on screen. Create an HTML overlay panel with: (1) pressure slider, (2) temperature slider, (3) a depth readout, (4) current buoyancy force readout, (5) a target depth indicator showing three sequential targets (y=15, y=8, y=3), and (6) a validation button that checks if lantern is within 0.5 units of current target AND turbulence is below 0.7 AND vertical velocity is below 0.1. On success, advance to next target with a green flash. Track and display completion of all three targets. The lantern should glow brighter at higher temperatures. Add bubble particles rising from the lantern proportional to gas release rate.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  lanternY: number,         // vertical position of lantern in scene units
  lanternVelY: number,         // vertical velocity of lantern from Euler integration
  gasPressure: number,         // current gas pressure slider value in atm (0.5-3.0)
  temperature: number,         // current temperature slider value in Kelvin (200-800)
  buoyantVolume: number,         // computed buoyant volume via ideal gas law PV=nRT
  fishY: number,         // vertical position of Barramundi fish
  fishX: number,         // horizontal position of fish along sinusoidal patrol
  lightIntensity: number,         // lantern point light intensity scaled by temperature
  neutralBuoyancy: boolean,         // true when lantern vertical velocity magnitude below threshold
  simTime: number,         // elapsed simulation time in seconds since start
}
